---
title: "Assignment 5B: Chess ELO Calculations"
format: html
---

## Introduction

In this assignment, I calculate each chess player’s expected score based on Elo rating differences against each opponent, then compare expected vs. actual performance. I also list the five players who most overperformed and the five who most underperformed.

## Setup
```{r} 
library(tidyverse)
```
```{R}
# Use a URL (GitHub raw link), not a local file path.
```

```{R}
# TODO: Implement expected score formula and compute expected score per matchup.
# Cite source for formula in the narrative text above/below.
```

```{R}
# TODO: Summarize expected totals and compare to actual totals.
# Output top 5 overperformers and top 5 underperformers.
```

```{R}
library(readr)

df <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/izzaikhan/DATA607-PROJECT-1/main/tournamentinfo.txt")
```

```{R}
glimpse(df)
head(df)
```

```{R}
# 1) load packages
library(tidyverse)

# 2) read the local file using pipe delimiter and trim whitespace
df_raw <- read_delim("tournamentinfo.txt",
                     delim = "|",
                     trim_ws = TRUE,
                     col_names = TRUE,
                     show_col_types = FALSE)

# 3) quick cleaning: remove leading/trailing spaces in column names and values
```

```{R}
library(readr)

df <- read_delim("tournamentinfo.txt",
                 delim = "|",
                 trim_ws = TRUE,
                 show_col_types = FALSE)

names(df)
head(df, 5)
```

```{R}
library(tibble)
library(stringr)

raw_lines <- readLines("tournamentinfo.txt", warn = FALSE)

# Remove divider lines like "-----"
raw_lines <- raw_lines[!grepl("^-{3,}$", str_trim(raw_lines))]

# Pick delimiter (try normal pipe first, else unicode pipe)
delim_used <- if (any(grepl("\\|", raw_lines))) "\\|" else "│"

spl <- strsplit(raw_lines, delim_used, perl = TRUE)
maxcols <- max(lengths(spl))

mat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(spl, function(x) c(x, rep(NA, maxcols - length(x)))))

df <- as_tibble(mat) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ str_trim(as.character(.))))

# If the first row looks like headers, use it
hdr <- as.character(unlist(df[1, ]))
names(df) <- make.names(hdr, unique = TRUE)
df <- df[-1, ]

names(df)
head(df, 5)
```

```{R}
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)

# 1) Add a row index so we can match each player row with the rating row under it
df2 <- df %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())

# 2) "Player rows" are the ones where Pair is a number
players <- df2 %>%
  filter(str_detect(Pair, "^\\d+$")) %>%
  mutate(pair_num = as.integer(Pair)) %>%
  select(row_id, pair_num, player_name = Player.Name, total_pts = Total,
         r1 = Round, r2 = Round.1, r3 = Round.2, r4 = Round.3, r5 = Round.4, r6 = Round.5, r7 = Round.6)

# 3) The "rating rows" are the lines right AFTER each player row (row_id + 1)
ratings <- df2 %>%
  filter(row_id %in% (players$row_id + 1)) %>%
  mutate(pair_num = players$pair_num) %>%
  transmute(
    pair_num,
    rating_text = Player.Name,
    pre_rating = as.integer(
      str_extract(
        str_extract(rating_text, "R:\\s*\\d+"),
        "\\d+"
      )
    )
  )
```

```{R}
head(ratings)
```

```{R}
players <- players %>%
  left_join(ratings, by = "pair_num") %>%
  rename(player_rating = pre_rating)

glimpse(players)
```

```{R}
games <- players %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches("^r\\d"),
    names_to = "round",
    values_to = "result_raw"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    result = str_extract(result_raw, "^[WDL]"),
    opp_num = as.integer(str_extract(result_raw, "\\d+")),
    actual_score = case_when(
      result == "W" ~ 1,
      result == "D" ~ 0.5,
      result == "L" ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(opp_num), !is.na(actual_score)) %>%
  select(
    player_num = pair_num,
    player_name,
    player_rating,
    round,
    opp_num,
    actual_score
  )

head(games, 10)
```

```{R}
opp_lookup <- players %>%
  select(
    opp_num = pair_num,
    opp_name = player_name,
    opp_rating = player_rating
  )

games <- games %>%
  left_join(opp_lookup, by = "opp_num")

head(games, 10)
```

```{R}
expected_score <- function(r_player, r_opp) {
  1 / (1 + 10 ^ ((r_opp - r_player) / 400))
}

games <- games %>%
  mutate(expected = expected_score(player_rating, opp_rating))

summary_tbl <- games %>%
  group_by(player_num, player_name, player_rating) %>%
  summarise(
    expected_total = sum(expected, na.rm = TRUE),
    actual_total = sum(actual_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    diff = actual_total - expected_total,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

top_over <- summary_tbl %>% arrange(desc(diff)) %>% slice_head(n = 5)
top_under <- summary_tbl %>% arrange(diff) %>% slice_head(n = 5)

top_over
top_under
```

I used the standard Elo expected-score formula.I computed expected score per matchup, summed expected totals per player, and compared them to actual totals (diff = actual − expected) to identify over- and under-performance.

```{R}
top_over
top_under
```
